youtube_items.title: [YT] NASA's SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Services Rendezvous and Docking
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] After a successful launch on May 15, 2026, from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida, a SpaceX Dragon cargo spacecraft will rendezvous with and dock to the International Space Station. The spacecraft, which is carrying about 6,500 pounds of cargo, is scheduled to dock autonomously at around 6:38 a.m. EDT (1038 UTC).

Dragon will deliver several new experiments, instruments and science investigations. These include a bone scaffold made from wood that could produce new treatments for frag

youtube_items.title: [YT] NASA's SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Services Launch
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] Watch as the 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission launches to the International Space Station. About 6,500 pounds of cargo are set to lift off at 6:05 p.m. EDT (2205 UTC) on May 15 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida atop a Falcon 9 rocket. Teams stood down from the previous launch opportunity on May 13 due to inclement weather.

In addition to cargo for the crew aboard the space station, Dragon will deliver several new experiments, instruments and sc

youtube_items.title: [YT] NASA's SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Services Launch (May 13, 2026 Attempt)
youtube_items.summary:
[DESCRIPTION FALLBACK] UPDATE: The May 13 launch attempt for CRS-34 was scrubbed due to weather: https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/spacestation/2026/05/13/nasa-spacex-target-may-15-for-resupply-mission-launch/ The next attempt is currently scheduled for Friday, May 15.

Watch as the 34th SpaceX commercial resupply mission launches to the International Space Station. About 6,500 pounds of cargo are set to lift off at 6:50 p.m. EDT (2250 UTC) on May 13 from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Flori

analysis.problem: Repeated weather-related delays in the SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Services mission are causing inefficiencies in the delivery of critical cargo to the International Space Station.
analysis.root_cause: The reliance on weather-dependent launch windows limits the flexibility and reliability of space cargo delivery, especially when multiple attempts are required due to unfavorable conditions.
leverage_points: Weather forecasting and real-time monitoring systems to reduce the number of scrubbed launches
Diversification of launch sites to minimize dependency on a single location

proposed_actions.action: Implement advanced weather prediction models and real-time atmospheric monitoring for launch windows
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce the number of scrubbed launches by 30% within the next six months

proposed_actions.action: Develop contingency launch sites in different geographic regions to mitigate weather-related delays
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Establish at least two additional launch sites with operational capability by 2028

evidence: SpaceX’s 34th Commercial Resupply Services mission was scrubbed on May 13 due to poor weather conditions.
The next launch attempt is scheduled for May 15, indicating a recurring issue with weather-dependent launch windows.
analysis.generalization: This issue reflects a broader challenge in space logistics where natural environmental factors can disrupt mission timelines and increase costs, impacting the reliability of space supply chains.
analysis.summary: Weather-related delays are causing inefficiencies in the SpaceX 34th resupply mission, highlighting the need for better weather forecasting and diversified launch capabilities.

raw_items.summary: Liftoff of the resupply mission, atop a Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for 6:05 p.m. EDT (2205 UTC). raw_items.summary: The static fire test was conducted by Northrop Grumman, the manufacturer of the GEM 63XL solid rocket booster that can be used to help power United Launch Alliance’s Vulcan rockets. raw_items.title: SpaceX targets May 19 for debut of Starship Version 3, Launch Pad 2
raw_items.summary: The mission will test a host of changes made to both the launch vehicle and the launch infrastructure as SpaceX prepares to support the Artemis 3 mission in 2027.

raw_items.title: For a second time, poor weather scrubs Cargo Dragon mission launch to the space station
raw_items.summary: SpaceX’s 34th mission as part of the Commercial Resupply Services 2 contract with NASA will deliver 6,500 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. Liftoff of the Falcon 9 rocket from pad 40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station is scheduled for Friday, May 15, at 6:05 p.m. EDT (2205 UTC). <a class="mh-excerpt-more" href="https://spaceflightnow.com/2026/05/12/live-coverage-nasa-spacex-to-launch-34th-cargo-dragon-mission-to-the-space-station/" title="For a second time, po

raw_items.title: Space exploration
raw_items.summary: Space exploration is the physical investigation of outer space by uncrewed robotic space probes and through human spaceflight.While the observation of objects in space, known as astronomy, predates reliable recorded history, it was the development of large and relatively efficient rockets during the mid-twentieth century that allowed physical space exploration to become a reality. raw_items.summary: April 17, 2026 - Space exploration, investigation, by means of crewed and uncrewed spacecraft, of the reaches of the universe beyond Earth’s atmosphere and the use of the information so gained to increase knowledge of the cosmos and benefit humanity. Learn more about space exploration in this article.

raw_items.title: SpaceX
raw_items.summary: Space Exploration Technologies Corporation, doing business as SpaceX, is a private American aerospace and artificial intelligence company headquartered at the Starbase development site in Starbase, Texas.
